content.problemStatement:
The GoC Timber Mafia is notorious for its deforestation
activities in the forests near Siruseri. These activities have
increased multifold after the death of the bandit who used to
lord over these jungles.  Having lost the battle to prevent the
Mafia from illegally felling the teak trees in this forest, the
government of Siruseri came up with a novel idea.  Why not
legalise the deforestation activity and at least make some money
in the process? So the Government decided to lease out parts of
the forest to the Timber Mafia.

Most of the teak trees in the forests of Siruseri were planted
during the colonial times, after the native trees had been cut.
Like everything European, the forest is very regular and
orderly. It is rectangular in shape and the trees are arranged in
uniformly spaced rows and coloumns.

Since the trees differ in height and girth, the timber value
differs from tree to tree.  The forest department has collected
data on each tree and knows the volume of wood (in cubic feet)
available in each tree in the forest.

The forest department maintains this information in the form of
an M×NM \times NM×N array of integers, where the
(i,j)(i, j)(i,j)th entry is the volume, in cubic feet, of the
ithi^{th}ith tree on the ithi^{th}ith row (or, equivalently, the
ithi^{th}ith tree on the ithi^{th}ith column).  We assume that
our rows are numbered top to bottom and the columns are numbered
from left to right.  For example, such an array could look like



This array tells us that  the volume of the tree at position (3,4)(3,4)(3,4) is 151515
cubic feet and so on.

Any rectangular piece of land with trees at each corner can be
leased out.  In order to fix the lease price for any rectangular
plot of the forest the forest department needs to know the amount
of wood available inside the plot.

A rectangular plot is described by the positions of the trees in
its top left corner and the bottom right corner. For example the
positions (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) and (3,4)(3,4)(3,4) describes the following part
rectangular part of the above forest.



The total amount of wood available in this rectangular plot is 767676
cubic feet.  Similarly (4,2)(4,2)(4,2) and (4,2)(4,2)(4,2) describes the rectangle
with just one tree and its volume is 202020 cubic feet.

Your task is to write a program that helps the forest department
to compute the total volume of the trees insides any specfied
rectangular plot.

Input:
The first line of the input contains two integers MMM and NNN indicating the number of rows and columns of trees in the forest.
The following MMM lines have NNN integers each. The jthj^{th}jth integer on line i+1i+1i+1 denotes the volume (in cubic feet) of the jthj^{th}jth  tree on the ithi^{th}ith  row.
Line M+2M+2M+2 contains a single integer CCC indicating the number of rectangles for which the total volume is to be computed.
Each of the following CCC lines (line M+2+1...M+2+CM+2+1 ... M+2+CM+2+1...M+2+C) contain four integers x1,y1,x2x_1, y_1, x_2x1​,y1​,x2​ and y2y_2y2​ (with x1≤x2x_1 \leq x_2x1​≤x2​ and y1≤y2y_1 \leq y_2y1​≤y2​) and describes a rectangle. The rectangle has its top left corner at the tree in position (x1,y1)(x_1,y_1)(x1​,y1​) and its bottom right corner at the tree at position (x2,y2)(x_2,y_2)(x2​,y2​).
Output:Your output must contain CCC lines with one integer on each line. Line iii must contain the total volume of wood in the rectangle described on line M+2+iM+2+iM+2+i in the input.
constraints: 0 ≤ volume of wood in each tree ≤ 1000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers M and N, representing the number of rows and columns of trees in the forest. The following M lines have N integers each, representing the volume of each tree. The next line contains an integer C, representing the number of rectangles for which the total volume is to be computed. Each of the following C lines contains four integers x1, y1, x2, and y2, representing the top-left and bottom-right corners of a rectangle.
content.outputFormat: The output contains C lines, each with a single integer representing the total volume of wood in the corresponding rectangle.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first rectangle has a total volume of 76, which is the sum of the volumes of the trees at positions (2,2), (2,3), (2,4), (3,2), (3,3), (3,4). The second rectangle has a total volume of 20, which is the volume of the tree at position (4,2).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a prefix sum array to store the cumulative sum of the volumes of the trees. This allows us to efficiently calculate the total volume of any rectangle in O(1) time. We first initialize the prefix sum array by iterating over each tree and adding its volume to the corresponding prefix sum. Then, for each query, we can calculate the total volume of the rectangle by subtracting the prefix sums of the top-left and bottom-right corners.
solution.timeComplexity: O(M*N + C) — The time complexity is dominated by the initialization of the prefix sum array, which takes O(M*N) time. The query time is O(1) per rectangle, so the total query time is O(C).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M*N) — The space complexity is dominated by the prefix sum array, which requires O(M*N) space.

code.Python3:
import sys
M, N = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
prefixSum = [[0] * (N + 1) for _ in range(M + 1)]
for i in range(1, M + 1):
    volumes = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    for j in range(1, N + 1):
        prefixSum[i][j] = prefixSum[i - 1][j] + prefixSum[i][j - 1] - prefixSum[i - 1][j - 1] + volumes[j - 1]
C = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(C):
    x1, y1, x2, y2 = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    totalVolume = prefixSum[x2][y2] - prefixSum[x1 - 1][y2] - prefixSum[x2][y1 - 1] + prefixSum[x1 - 1][y1 - 1]
    print(totalVolume)
